1→description = """
     2→Find and reassign orphaned work.
     3→
     4→Dogs work through molecules (poured from this formula) to scan for orphaned state:
     5→- Issues marked in_progress with no active polecat
     6→- Molecules attached but worker gone
     7→- Merge queue entries with dead owners
     8→- Wisps from terminated sessions
     9→
    10→This is a cleanup and recovery formula. Found orphans are either:
    11→- Reassigned to available workers
    12→- Reset to open status for next dispatch
    13→- Escalated if data loss occurred
    14→
    15→## Dog Contract
    16→
    17→This is infrastructure work. You:
    18→1. Receive scan scope via hook_bead (rig or town-wide)
    19→2. Scan for orphaned state
    20→3. Classify and triage orphans
    21→4. Take recovery action
    22→5. Report findings
    23→6. Return to kennel
    24→
    25→## Variables
    26→
    27→| Variable | Source | Description |
    28→|----------|--------|-------------|
    29→| scope | hook_bead | Scan scope: 'town' or specific rig name |
    30→
    31→## Why Dogs?
    32→
    33→Orphan scanning requires multi-rig access and may need to interact with
    34→multiple Witnesses. Dogs have the cross-rig worktrees needed for this."""
    35→formula = "mol-orphan-scan"
    36→version = 1
    37→
    38→[squash]
    39→trigger = "on_complete"
    40→template_type = "work"
    41→include_metrics = true
    42→
    43→[[steps]]
    44→id = "determine-scope"
    45→title = "Determine scan scope"
    46→description = """
    47→Establish what to scan for orphans.
    48→
    49→**1. Check assignment:**
    50→```bash
    51→gt hook               # Shows scope in hook_bead
    52→```
    53→
    54→**2. Resolve scope:**
    55→- 'town': Scan all rigs
    56→- '<rig>': Scan specific rig only
    57→
    58→```bash
    59→# If town-wide
    60→gt rigs                     # Get list of all rigs
    61→
    62→# If specific rig
    63→# Just use that rig
    64→```
    65→
    66→**Exit criteria:** Scope determined, rig list established."""
    67→
    68→[[steps]]
    69→id = "scan-orphaned-issues"
    70→title = "Scan for orphaned issues"
    71→needs = ["determine-scope"]
    72→description = """
    73→Find issues marked in_progress with no active worker.
    74→
    75→**1. For each rig in scope, find in_progress issues:**
    76→```bash
    77→bd list --status=in_progress
    78→```
    79→
    80→**2. For each in_progress issue, check assignee:**
    81→```bash
    82→bd show <issue-id>
    83→# Get assignee field
    84→```
    85→
    86→**3. Check if assignee session exists:**
    87→```bash
    88→# If assignee is a polecat
    89→gt polecats <rig>           # Is the polecat active?
    90→tmux has-session -t <session> 2>/dev/null
    91→```
    92→
    93→**4. Identify orphans:**
    94→- Issue in_progress + assignee session dead = orphan
    95→- Issue in_progress + no assignee = orphan
    96→
    97→Record each orphan with:
    98→- Issue ID
    99→- Last assignee (if any)
   100→- How long orphaned (last update timestamp)
   101→
   102→**Exit criteria:** Orphaned issues identified."""
   103→
   104→[[steps]]
   105→id = "scan-orphaned-molecules"
   106→title = "Scan for orphaned molecules"
   107→needs = ["determine-scope"]
   108→description = """
   109→Find molecules attached to dead sessions.
   110→
   111→**1. List active molecules:**
   112→```bash
   113→bd mol list --active
   114→```
   115→
   116→**2. For each molecule, check owner session:**
   117→```bash
   118→bd mol show <mol-id>
   119→# Get agent/session info
   120→```
   121→
   122→**3. Check if owner session exists:**
   123→```bash
   124→tmux has-session -t <session> 2>/dev/null
   125→```
   126→
   127→**4. Identify orphans:**
   128→- Molecule in_progress + owner session dead = orphan
   129→- Molecule hooked + owner session dead = orphan
   130→
   131→Record each orphan for triage.
   132→
   133→**Exit criteria:** Orphaned molecules identified."""
   134→
   135→[[steps]]
   136→id = "scan-orphaned-wisps"
   137→title = "Scan for orphaned wisps"
   138→needs = ["determine-scope"]
   139→description = """
   140→Find wisps from terminated sessions.
   141→
   142→**1. List wisps in ephemeral storage:**
   143→```bash
   144→ls .beads-wisp/             # Or equivalent location
   145→```
   146→
   147→**2. For each wisp, check spawner session:**
   148→Wisps should have metadata indicating the spawning session.
   149→
   150→**3. Identify orphans:**
   151→- Wisp age > 1 hour + spawner session dead = orphan
   152→- Wisp with no spawner metadata = orphan
   153→
   154→**4. Check for unsquashed content:**
   155→Orphaned wisps may have audit-worthy content that wasn't squashed.
   156→
   157→**Exit criteria:** Orphaned wisps identified."""
   158→
   159→[[steps]]
   160→id = "triage-orphans"
   161→title = "Classify and triage orphans"
   162→needs = ["scan-orphaned-issues", "scan-orphaned-molecules", "scan-orphaned-wisps"]
   163→description = """
   164→Classify orphans by severity and determine action.
   165→
   166→**1. Classify by type:**
   167→
   168→| Type | Severity | Typical Action |
   169→|------|----------|----------------|
   170→| Issue in_progress, no work done | Low | Reset to open |
   171→| Issue in_progress, work in progress | Medium | Check branch, reassign |
   172→| Molecule mid-execution | Medium | Resume or restart |
   173→| Wisp with content | Low | Squash or burn |
   174→| Wisp empty | None | Delete |
   175→
   176→**2. Check for data loss:**
   177→For issues/molecules with possible work:
   178→```bash
   179→# Check for branch with work
   180→git branch -a | grep <polecat-or-issue>
   181→git log --oneline <branch>
   182→```
   183→
   184→**3. Categorize for action:**
   185→- RESET: Return to open status for normal dispatch
   186→- REASSIGN: Assign to specific worker immediately
   187→- RECOVER: Salvage work from branch/state
   188→- ESCALATE: Data loss or complex situation
   189→- BURN: Safe to delete (empty wisps, etc.)
   190→
   191→**Exit criteria:** All orphans categorized with planned action."""
   192→
   193→[[steps]]
   194→id = "execute-recovery"
   195→title = "Execute recovery actions"
   196→needs = ["triage-orphans"]
   197→description = """
   198→Take action on each orphan based on triage.
   199→
   200→**1. RESET orphans:**
   201→```bash
   202→bd update <issue> --status=open --assignee=""
   203→# Clears in_progress, ready for dispatch
   204→```
   205→
   206→**2. REASSIGN orphans:**
   207→```bash
   208→# Notify Witness to handle assignment
   209→gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "Orphan needs assignment: <issue>" \
   210→  -m "Issue <id> was orphaned. Has partial work. Needs reassignment."
   211→```
   212→
   213→**3. RECOVER orphans:**
   214→```bash
   215→# For issues with work on branch:
   216→# - Preserve the branch
   217→# - Create recovery note
   218→bd update <issue> --status=open \
   219→  --note="Recovery: work exists on branch <branch>"
   220→```
   221→
   222→**4. ESCALATE orphans:**
   223→```bash
   224→gt mail send mayor/ -s "Orphan requires escalation: <issue>" \
   225→  -m "Issue <id> orphaned with possible data loss.
   226→Details: ...
   227→Recommended action: ..."
   228→```
   229→
   230→**5. BURN orphans:**
   231→```bash
   232→# For empty wisps, etc.
   233→rm .beads-wisp/<wisp-file>
   234→```
   235→
   236→**Exit criteria:** All orphans handled."""
   237→
   238→[[steps]]
   239→id = "report-findings"
   240→title = "Generate and send orphan report"
   241→needs = ["execute-recovery"]
   242→description = """
   243→Create summary report of orphan scan and actions.
   244→
   245→**1. Generate report:**
   246→```markdown
   247→## Orphan Scan Report: {{timestamp}}
   248→
   249→**Scope**: {{scope}}
   250→**Orphans found**: {{total_count}}
   251→
   252→### By Type
   253→- Issues: {{issue_count}}
   254→- Molecules: {{mol_count}}
   255→- Wisps: {{wisp_count}}
   256→
   257→### Actions Taken
   258→- Reset to open: {{reset_count}}
   259→- Reassigned: {{reassign_count}}
   260→- Recovered: {{recover_count}}
   261→- Escalated: {{escalate_count}}
   262→- Burned: {{burn_count}}
   263→
   264→### Details
   265→{{#each orphan}}
   266→- {{type}} {{id}}: {{action}} - {{reason}}
   267→{{/each}}
   268→```
   269→
   270→**2. Send to Deacon (for logs):**
   271→```bash
   272→gt mail send deacon/ -s "Orphan scan complete: {{total_count}} found" \
   273→  -m "{{report}}"
   274→```
   275→
   276→**3. Send to Mayor (if escalations):**
   277→```bash
   278→# Only if there were escalations
   279→gt mail send mayor/ -s "Orphan scan: {{escalate_count}} escalations" \
   280→  -m "{{escalations_section}}"
   281→```
   282→
   283→**Exit criteria:** Reports sent."""
   284→
   285→[[steps]]
   286→id = "return-to-kennel"
   287→title = "Signal completion and return to kennel"
   288→needs = ["report-findings"]
   289→description = """
   290→Signal work complete and return to available pool.
   291→
   292→**1. Signal completion to Deacon:**
   293→```bash
   294→gt mail send deacon/ -s "DOG_DONE $(hostname)" -m "Task: orphan-scan
   295→Scope: {{scope}}
   296→Orphans found: {{total_count}}
   297→Actions taken: {{action_summary}}
   298→Status: COMPLETE
   299→
   300→Ready for next assignment."
   301→```
   302→
   303→**2. Return to kennel:**
   304→Dog returns to available state in the pool.
   305→
   306→**Exit criteria:** Deacon notified, dog ready for next work."""
   307→
   308→[vars]
   309→[vars.scope]
   310→description = "Scan scope: 'town' or specific rig name"
   311→required = true
   312→default = "town"
   313→

1→description = """
     2→Clean stale sessions and garbage collect.
     3→
     4→Dogs work through molecules (poured from this formula) to clean up dead sessions, orphaned processes, and
     5→other system cruft. This is the garbage collector for Gas Town's runtime:
     6→- Dead tmux sessions (no Claude process)
     7→- Orphaned Claude processes (no tmux parent)
     8→- Stale wisps past retention
     9→- Leftover state files
    10→
    11→## Dog Contract
    12→
    13→This is infrastructure work. You:
    14→1. Receive gc scope via hook_bead (aggressive or conservative)
    15→2. Identify garbage across the system
    16→3. Safely remove dead state
    17→4. Report what was cleaned
    18→5. Return to kennel
    19→
    20→## Variables
    21→
    22→| Variable | Source | Description |
    23→|----------|--------|-------------|
    24→| mode | hook_bead | GC mode: 'conservative' (safe) or 'aggressive' (thorough) |
    25→
    26→## Safety
    27→
    28→GC is destructive. This formula errs on the side of caution:
    29→- Conservative mode: Only obviously dead things
    30→- Aggressive mode: Includes old but possibly-recoverable state
    31→
    32→Running `gt doctor --fix` handles most of this. This formula wraps it with
    33→reporting and multi-rig scope."""
    34→formula = "mol-session-gc"
    35→version = 1
    36→
    37→[squash]
    38→trigger = "on_complete"
    39→template_type = "work"
    40→include_metrics = true
    41→
    42→[[steps]]
    43→id = "determine-mode"
    44→title = "Determine GC mode"
    45→description = """
    46→Establish GC aggressiveness level.
    47→
    48→**1. Check assignment:**
    49→```bash
    50→gt hook               # Shows mode in hook_bead
    51→```
    52→
    53→**2. Mode definitions:**
    54→
    55→| Mode | Description | Risk |
    56→|------|-------------|------|
    57→| conservative | Only clearly dead state | Very low |
    58→| aggressive | Includes stale state | Low but non-zero |
    59→
    60→**Conservative targets:**
    61→- tmux sessions with no processes
    62→- Claude processes with no tmux parent
    63→- Wisps > 24 hours old
    64→
    65→**Aggressive additions:**
    66→- Wisps > 1 hour old
    67→- Branches with no matching polecat
    68→- State files > 7 days old
    69→
    70→**Exit criteria:** GC mode determined."""
    71→
    72→[[steps]]
    73→id = "preview-cleanup"
    74→title = "Preview what will be cleaned"
    75→needs = ["determine-mode"]
    76→description = """
    77→Identify garbage without removing it yet.
    78→
    79→**1. Run doctor in preview mode:**
    80→```bash
    81→gt doctor -v
    82→# Shows what would be cleaned, doesn't do it
    83→```
    84→
    85→**2. Parse doctor output for:**
    86→- orphan-sessions: Tmux sessions to kill
    87→- orphan-processes: Claude processes to terminate
    88→- wisp-gc: Wisps to delete
    89→
    90→**3. Additional scans (for aggressive mode):**
    91→```bash
    92→# Old branches
    93→git branch --list 'polecat/*' | while read branch; do
    94→  last_commit=$(git log -1 --format=%ct "$branch")
    95→  # If > 7 days old and no matching polecat, candidate for cleanup
    96→done
    97→
    98→# Old state files
    99→find ~/.gt/ -name "*.state" -mtime +7
   100→```
   101→
   102→**4. Compile cleanup manifest:**
   103→Record each item to be cleaned with:
   104→- Type (session, process, wisp, branch, state)
   105→- Identifier
   106→- Age
   107→- Reason for cleanup
   108→
   109→**Exit criteria:** Cleanup manifest ready, nothing deleted yet."""
   110→
   111→[[steps]]
   112→id = "execute-gc"
   113→title = "Execute garbage collection"
   114→needs = ["preview-cleanup"]
   115→description = """
   116→Actually remove the garbage.
   117→
   118→**1. Run doctor with fix:**
   119→```bash
   120→gt doctor --fix
   121→# This handles sessions, processes, and wisps
   122→```
   123→
   124→**2. For aggressive mode, additional cleanup:**
   125→```bash
   126→# Old branches (if aggressive mode)
   127→git branch -D <old-branch>
   128→
   129→# Old state files (if aggressive mode)
   130→rm <state-file>
   131→```
   132→
   133→**3. Track what was deleted:**
   134→Record each item actually removed for the report.
   135→
   136→**Safety checks:**
   137→- Never delete active sessions (tmux list-clients)
   138→- Never delete branches with uncommitted polecat work
   139→- Never delete wisps < 1 hour old
   140→
   141→**Exit criteria:** Garbage collected."""
   142→
   143→[[steps]]
   144→id = "verify-cleanup"
   145→title = "Verify cleanup was successful"
   146→needs = ["execute-gc"]
   147→description = """
   148→Confirm garbage was actually removed.
   149→
   150→**1. Re-run doctor to verify:**
   151→```bash
   152→gt doctor -v
   153→# Should show no issues (or fewer issues)
   154→```
   155→
   156→**2. Check for stragglers:**
   157→```bash
   158→# Tmux sessions
   159→tmux list-sessions 2>/dev/null
   160→
   161→# Claude processes
   162→pgrep -f claude
   163→
   164→# Wisps
   165→ls .beads-wisp/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l
   166→```
   167→
   168→**3. Compare before/after:**
   169→- Sessions before: N → after: M
   170→- Processes before: N → after: M
   171→- Wisps before: N → after: M
   172→
   173→**Exit criteria:** Cleanup verified."""
   174→
   175→[[steps]]
   176→id = "report-gc"
   177→title = "Generate GC report"
   178→needs = ["verify-cleanup"]
   179→description = """
   180→Create summary report of garbage collection.
   181→
   182→**1. Generate report:**
   183→```markdown
   184→## Session GC Report: {{timestamp}}
   185→
   186→**Mode**: {{mode}}
   187→
   188→### Summary
   189→| Type | Before | After | Cleaned |
   190→|------|--------|-------|---------|
   191→| Sessions | X | Y | Z |
   192→| Processes | X | Y | Z |
   193→| Wisps | X | Y | Z |
   194→| Branches | X | Y | Z |
   195→| State files | X | Y | Z |
   196→
   197→### Items Cleaned
   198→{{#each cleaned}}
   199→- {{type}}: {{identifier}} (age: {{age}}, reason: {{reason}})
   200→{{/each}}
   201→
   202→### Errors
   203→{{#if errors}}
   204→{{#each errors}}
   205→- {{item}}: {{error}}
   206→{{/each}}
   207→{{else}}
   208→None
   209→{{/if}}
   210→
   211→### Space Recovered
   212→~{{bytes_freed}} bytes
   213→```
   214→
   215→**2. Send to Deacon:**
   216→```bash
   217→gt mail send deacon/ -s "GC complete: {{total_cleaned}} items" \
   218→  -m "{{report}}"
   219→```
   220→
   221→**Exit criteria:** Report sent."""
   222→
   223→[[steps]]
   224→id = "return-to-kennel"
   225→title = "Signal completion and return to kennel"
   226→needs = ["report-gc"]
   227→description = """
   228→Signal work complete and return to available pool.
   229→
   230→**1. Signal completion to Deacon:**
   231→```bash
   232→gt mail send deacon/ -s "DOG_DONE $(hostname)" -m "Task: session-gc
   233→Mode: {{mode}}
   234→Items cleaned: {{total_cleaned}}
   235→Space recovered: {{bytes_freed}}
   236→Status: COMPLETE
   237→
   238→Ready for next assignment."
   239→```
   240→
   241→**2. Return to kennel:**
   242→Dog returns to available state in the pool.
   243→
   244→**Exit criteria:** Deacon notified, dog ready for next work."""
   245→
   246→[vars]
   247→[vars.mode]
   248→description = "GC mode: 'conservative' or 'aggressive'"
   249→required = true
   250→default = "conservative"
   251→

1→description = """
     2→Death warrant execution state machine for Dogs.
     3→
     4→Dogs execute this molecule to process death warrants. Each Dog is a lightweight
     5→goroutine (NOT a Claude session) that runs the interrogation state machine.
     6→
     7→## Architecture Context
     8→
     9→Dogs are lightweight workers in Boot's pool (see dog-pool-architecture.md):
    10→- Fixed pool of 5 goroutines (configurable via GT_DOG_POOL_SIZE)
    11→- State persisted to $GT_ROOT/deacon/dogs/active/<id>.json
    12→- Recovery on Boot restart via orphan state files
    13→
    14→## State Machine
    15→
    16→```
    17→                    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
    18→                    │                                         │
    19→                    ▼                                         │
    20→    ┌───────────────────────────┐                            │
    21→    │       INTERROGATING       │                            │
    22→    │                           │                            │
    23→    │  1. Send health check     │                            │
    24→    │  2. Open timeout gate     │                            │
    25→    └───────────┬───────────────┘                            │
    26→                │                                             │
    27→                │ gate closes (timeout or response)           │
    28→                ▼                                             │
    29→    ┌───────────────────────────┐                            │
    30→    │       EVALUATING          │                            │
    31→    │                           │                            │
    32→    │  Check tmux output for    │                            │
    33→    │  ALIVE keyword            │                            │
    34→    └───────────┬───────────────┘                            │
    35→                │                                             │
    36→        ┌───────┴───────┐                                    │
    37→        │               │                                    │
    38→        ▼               ▼                                    │
    39→   [ALIVE found]   [No ALIVE]                               │
    40→        │               │                                    │
    41→        │               │ attempt < 3?                       │
    42→        │               ├──────────────────────────────────→─┘
    43→        │               │ yes: attempt++, longer timeout
    44→        │               │
    45→        │               │ no: attempt == 3
    46→        ▼               ▼
    47→    ┌─────────┐    ┌─────────────┐
    48→    │ PARDONED│    │  EXECUTING  │
    49→    │         │    │             │
    50→    │ Cancel  │    │ Kill tmux   │
    51→    │ warrant │    │ session     │
    52→    └────┬────┘    └──────┬──────┘
    53→         │                │
    54→         └────────┬───────┘
    55→                  │
    56→                  ▼
    57→         ┌────────────────┐
    58→         │    EPITAPH     │
    59→         │                │
    60→         │  Log outcome   │
    61→         │  Release dog   │
    62→         └────────────────┘
    63→```
    64→
    65→## Timeout Gates
    66→
    67→| Attempt | Timeout | Cumulative Wait |
    68→|---------|---------|-----------------|
    69→| 1       | 60s     | 60s             |
    70→| 2       | 120s    | 180s (3 min)    |
    71→| 3       | 240s    | 420s (7 min)    |
    72→
    73→Timeout gates work like this:
    74→- Gate opens when interrogation message is sent
    75→- Gate closes when EITHER:
    76→  a) Timeout expires (proceed to evaluate)
    77→  b) Response detected (early close, proceed to evaluate)
    78→- The gate state determines the evaluation outcome
    79→
    80→## Interrogation Message Format
    81→
    82→```
    83→[DOG] HEALTH CHECK: Session {target}, respond ALIVE within {timeout}s or face termination.
    84→Warrant reason: {reason}
    85→Filed by: {requester}
    86→Attempt: {attempt}/3
    87→```
    88→
    89→## Response Detection
    90→
    91→The Dog checks tmux output for:
    92→1. The ALIVE keyword (explicit response)
    93→2. Any Claude output after the health check (implicit activity)
    94→
    95→```go
    96→func (d *Dog) CheckForResponse() bool {
    97→    output := tmux.CapturePane(d.Warrant.Target, 50) // Last 50 lines
    98→    return strings.Contains(output, "ALIVE")
    99→}
   100→```
   101→
   102→## Variables
   103→
   104→| Variable    | Source      | Description                                   |
   105→|-------------|-------------|-----------------------------------------------|
   106→| warrant_id  | hook_bead   | Bead ID of the death warrant                  |
   107→| target      | warrant     | Session name to interrogate                   |
   108→| reason      | warrant     | Why warrant was issued                        |
   109→| requester   | warrant     | Who filed the warrant (e.g., deacon, witness) |
   110→
   111→## Integration
   112→
   113→Dogs are NOT Claude sessions. This molecule is:
   114→1. A specification document (defines the state machine)
   115→2. A reference for Go implementation in internal/shutdown/
   116→3. A template for creating warrant-tracking beads
   117→
   118→The Go implementation follows this spec exactly."""
   119→formula = "mol-shutdown-dance"
   120→version = 1
   121→
   122→[squash]
   123→trigger = "on_complete"
   124→template_type = "operational"
   125→include_metrics = true
   126→
   127→# ============================================================================
   128→# STEP 1: WARRANT_RECEIVED
   129→# ============================================================================
   130→[[steps]]
   131→id = "warrant-received"
   132→title = "Receive and validate death warrant"
   133→description = """
   134→Entry point when Dog is allocated from pool.
   135→
   136→**1. Read warrant from allocation:**
   137→The Dog receives a Warrant struct containing:
   138→- ID: Bead ID of the warrant
   139→- Target: Session name (e.g., "gt-gastown-Toast")
   140→- Reason: Why termination requested
   141→- Requester: Who filed (deacon, witness, mayor)
   142→- FiledAt: Timestamp
   143→
   144→**2. Validate target exists:**
   145→```bash
   146→tmux has-session -t {target} 2>/dev/null
   147→```
   148→
   149→If target doesn't exist:
   150→- Warrant is stale (already dead)
   151→- Skip to EPITAPH with outcome=already_dead
   152→
   153→**3. Initialize state file:**
   154→Write initial state to $GT_ROOT/deacon/dogs/active/{dog-id}.json
   155→
   156→**4. Set initial attempt counter:**
   157→attempt = 1
   158→
   159→**Exit criteria:** Warrant validated, target confirmed alive, state initialized."""
   160→
   161→# ============================================================================
   162→# STEP 2: INTERROGATION_1 (60s timeout)
   163→# ============================================================================
   164→[[steps]]
   165→id = "interrogation-1"
   166→title = "First interrogation (60s timeout)"
   167→needs = ["warrant-received"]
   168→description = """
   169→First attempt to contact the session.
   170→
   171→**1. Compose health check message:**
   172→```
   173→[DOG] HEALTH CHECK: Session {target}, respond ALIVE within 60s or face termination.
   174→Warrant reason: {reason}
   175→Filed by: {requester}
   176→Attempt: 1/3
   177→```
   178→
   179→**2. Send via tmux:**
   180→```bash
   181→tmux send-keys -t {target} "{message}" Enter
   182→```
   183→
   184→**3. Open timeout gate:**
   185→Gate configuration:
   186→- Type: timer
   187→- Timeout: 60 seconds
   188→- Close conditions:
   189→  a) Timer expires
   190→  b) ALIVE keyword detected in output
   191→
   192→**4. Wait for gate to close:**
   193→The Dog waits (select on timer channel or early close signal).
   194→
   195→**5. Record interrogation timestamp:**
   196→Update state file with last_message_at.
   197→
   198→**Exit criteria:** Message sent, waiting for gate to close."""
   199→
   200→# ============================================================================
   201→# STEP 3: EVALUATE_1
   202→# ============================================================================
   203→[[steps]]
   204→id = "evaluate-1"
   205→title = "Evaluate first interrogation response"
   206→needs = ["interrogation-1"]
   207→description = """
   208→Check if session responded to first interrogation.
   209→
   210→**1. Capture tmux output:**
   211→```bash
   212→tmux capture-pane -t {target} -p | tail -50
   213→```
   214→
   215→**2. Check for ALIVE keyword:**
   216→```go
   217→if strings.Contains(output, "ALIVE") {
   218→    return PARDONED
   219→}
   220→```
   221→
   222→**3. Decision:**
   223→- ALIVE found → Proceed to PARDON
   224→- No ALIVE → Proceed to INTERROGATION_2
   225→
   226→**Exit criteria:** Response evaluated, next step determined."""
   227→
   228→# ============================================================================
   229→# STEP 4: INTERROGATION_2 (120s timeout)
   230→# ============================================================================
   231→[[steps]]
   232→id = "interrogation-2"
   233→title = "Second interrogation (120s timeout)"
   234→needs = ["evaluate-1"]
   235→gate = { type = "conditional", condition = "no_response_1" }
   236→description = """
   237→Second attempt with longer timeout.
   238→
   239→Only executed if evaluate-1 found no response.
   240→
   241→**1. Increment attempt:**
   242→attempt = 2
   243→
   244→**2. Compose health check message:**
   245→```
   246→[DOG] HEALTH CHECK: Session {target}, respond ALIVE within 120s or face termination.
   247→Warrant reason: {reason}
   248→Filed by: {requester}
   249→Attempt: 2/3
   250→```
   251→
   252→**3. Send via tmux:**
   253→```bash
   254→tmux send-keys -t {target} "{message}" Enter
   255→```
   256→
   257→**4. Open timeout gate:**
   258→- Type: timer
   259→- Timeout: 120 seconds
   260→
   261→**5. Wait for gate to close.**
   262→
   263→**Exit criteria:** Second message sent, waiting for gate."""
   264→
   265→# ============================================================================
   266→# STEP 5: EVALUATE_2
   267→# ============================================================================
   268→[[steps]]
   269→id = "evaluate-2"
   270→title = "Evaluate second interrogation response"
   271→needs = ["interrogation-2"]
   272→description = """
   273→Check if session responded to second interrogation.
   274→
   275→**1. Capture tmux output:**
   276→```bash
   277→tmux capture-pane -t {target} -p | tail -50
   278→```
   279→
   280→**2. Check for ALIVE keyword.**
   281→
   282→**3. Decision:**
   283→- ALIVE found → Proceed to PARDON
   284→- No ALIVE → Proceed to INTERROGATION_3
   285→
   286→**Exit criteria:** Response evaluated, next step determined."""
   287→
   288→# ============================================================================
   289→# STEP 6: INTERROGATION_3 (240s timeout)
   290→# ============================================================================
   291→[[steps]]
   292→id = "interrogation-3"
   293→title = "Final interrogation (240s timeout)"
   294→needs = ["evaluate-2"]
   295→gate = { type = "conditional", condition = "no_response_2" }
   296→description = """
   297→Final attempt before execution.
   298→
   299→Only executed if evaluate-2 found no response.
   300→
   301→**1. Increment attempt:**
   302→attempt = 3
   303→
   304→**2. Compose health check message:**
   305→```
   306→[DOG] HEALTH CHECK: Session {target}, respond ALIVE within 240s or face termination.
   307→Warrant reason: {reason}
   308→Filed by: {requester}
   309→Attempt: 3/3
   310→```
   311→
   312→**3. Send via tmux:**
   313→```bash
   314→tmux send-keys -t {target} "{message}" Enter
   315→```
   316→
   317→**4. Open timeout gate:**
   318→- Type: timer
   319→- Timeout: 240 seconds
   320→- This is the FINAL chance
   321→
   322→**5. Wait for gate to close.**
   323→
   324→**Exit criteria:** Final message sent, waiting for gate."""
   325→
   326→# ============================================================================
   327→# STEP 7: EVALUATE_3
   328→# ============================================================================
   329→[[steps]]
   330→id = "evaluate-3"
   331→title = "Evaluate final interrogation response"
   332→needs = ["interrogation-3"]
   333→description = """
   334→Final evaluation before execution.
   335→
   336→**1. Capture tmux output:**
   337→```bash
   338→tmux capture-pane -t {target} -p | tail -50
   339→```
   340→
   341→**2. Check for ALIVE keyword.**
   342→
   343→**3. Decision:**
   344→- ALIVE found → Proceed to PARDON
   345→- No ALIVE → Proceed to EXECUTE
   346→
   347→**Exit criteria:** Final decision made."""
   348→
   349→# ============================================================================
   350→# STEP 8: PARDON (success path)
   351→# ============================================================================
   352→[[steps]]
   353→id = "pardon"
   354→title = "Pardon session - cancel warrant"
   355→needs = ["evaluate-1", "evaluate-2", "evaluate-3"]
   356→gate = { type = "conditional", condition = "alive_detected" }
   357→description = """
   358→Session responded - cancel the death warrant.
   359→
   360→**1. Update state:**
   361→state = PARDONED
   362→
   363→**2. Record pardon details:**
   364→```json
   365→{
   366→  "outcome": "pardoned",
   367→  "attempt": {attempt},
   368→  "response_time": "{time_since_last_interrogation}s",
   369→  "pardoned_at": "{timestamp}"
   370→}
   371→```
   372→
   373→**3. Cancel warrant bead:**
   374→```bash
   375→bd close {warrant_id} --reason "Session responded at attempt {attempt}"
   376→```
   377→
   378→**4. Notify requester:**
   379→```bash
   380→gt mail send {requester}/ -s "PARDON: {target}" -m "Death warrant cancelled.
   381→Session responded after attempt {attempt}.
   382→Warrant: {warrant_id}
   383→Response detected: {timestamp}"
   384→```
   385→
   386→**Exit criteria:** Warrant cancelled, requester notified."""
   387→
   388→# ============================================================================
   389→# STEP 9: EXECUTE (termination path)
   390→# ============================================================================
   391→[[steps]]
   392→id = "execute"
   393→title = "Execute warrant - kill session"
   394→needs = ["evaluate-3"]
   395→gate = { type = "conditional", condition = "no_response_final" }
   396→description = """
   397→Session unresponsive after 3 attempts - execute the warrant.
   398→
   399→**1. Update state:**
   400→state = EXECUTING
   401→
   402→**2. Kill the tmux session:**
   403→```bash
   404→tmux kill-session -t {target}
   405→```
   406→
   407→**3. Verify session is dead:**
   408→```bash
   409→tmux has-session -t {target} 2>/dev/null
   410→# Should fail (session gone)
   411→```
   412→
   413→**4. If session still exists (kill failed):**
   414→- Force kill with tmux kill-server if isolated
   415→- Or escalate to Boot for manual intervention
   416→
   417→**5. Record execution details:**
   418→```json
   419→{
   420→  "outcome": "executed",
   421→  "attempts": 3,
   422→  "total_wait": "420s",
   423→  "executed_at": "{timestamp}"
   424→}
   425→```
   426→
   427→**Exit criteria:** Session terminated."""
   428→
   429→# ============================================================================
   430→# STEP 10: EPITAPH (completion)
   431→# ============================================================================
   432→[[steps]]
   433→id = "epitaph"
   434→title = "Log cause of death and close warrant"
   435→needs = ["pardon", "execute"]
   436→description = """
   437→Final step - create audit record and release Dog back to pool.
   438→
   439→**1. Compose epitaph based on outcome:**
   440→
   441→For PARDONED:
   442→```
   443→EPITAPH: {target}
   444→Verdict: PARDONED
   445→Warrant: {warrant_id}
   446→Reason: {reason}
   447→Filed by: {requester}
   448→Response: Attempt {attempt}, after {wait_time}s
   449→Pardoned at: {timestamp}
   450→```
   451→
   452→For EXECUTED:
   453→```
   454→EPITAPH: {target}
   455→Verdict: EXECUTED
   456→Warrant: {warrant_id}
   457→Reason: {reason}
   458→Filed by: {requester}
   459→Attempts: 3 (60s + 120s + 240s = 420s total)
   460→Executed at: {timestamp}
   461→```
   462→
   463→For ALREADY_DEAD (target gone before interrogation):
   464→```
   465→EPITAPH: {target}
   466→Verdict: ALREADY_DEAD
   467→Warrant: {warrant_id}
   468→Reason: {reason}
   469→Filed by: {requester}
   470→Note: Target session not found at warrant processing
   471→```
   472→
   473→**2. Close warrant bead:**
   474→```bash
   475→bd close {warrant_id} --reason "{epitaph_summary}"
   476→```
   477→
   478→**3. Move state file to completed:**
   479→```bash
   480→mv $GT_ROOT/deacon/dogs/active/{dog-id}.json $GT_ROOT/deacon/dogs/completed/
   481→```
   482→
   483→**4. Report to Boot:**
   484→Write completion file: $GT_ROOT/deacon/dogs/active/{dog-id}.done
   485→```json
   486→{
   487→  "dog_id": "{dog-id}",
   488→  "warrant_id": "{warrant_id}",
   489→  "target": "{target}",
   490→  "outcome": "{pardoned|executed|already_dead}",
   491→  "duration": "{total_duration}s"
   492→}
   493→```
   494→
   495→**5. Release Dog to pool:**
   496→Dog resets state and returns to idle channel.
   497→
   498→**Exit criteria:** Warrant closed, Dog released, audit complete."""
   499→
   500→# ============================================================================
   501→# VARIABLES
   502→# ============================================================================
   503→[vars]
   504→[vars.warrant_id]
   505→description = "Bead ID of the death warrant being processed"
   506→required = true
   507→
   508→[vars.target]
   509→description = "Session name to interrogate (e.g., gt-gastown-Toast)"
   510→required = true
   511→
   512→[vars.reason]
   513→description = "Why the warrant was issued"
   514→required = true
   515→
   516→[vars.requester]
   517→description = "Who filed the warrant (deacon, witness, mayor)"
   518→required = true
   519→default = "deacon"
   520→

1→description = """
     2→Workspace synchronization molecule for batch prep.
     3→
     4→This molecule prepares an agent's workspace for a new batch of work. It syncs git
     5→and beads state, cleans up cruft, verifies the baseline is healthy, and reports
     6→readiness. Designed to be broadcast to all agents when preparing for coordinated
     7→work.
     8→
     9→## When to Use
    10→
    11→- Before major batch assignments
    12→- After extended idle periods
    13→- When repo baseline has diverged significantly
    14→- Proactive cleanup between work sessions
    15→
    16→## Conflict Resolution Philosophy
    17→
    18→**Preserve and re-land, not discard.**
    19→
    20→When git pull/rebase hits conflicts, the agent should:
    21→1. Assess if the conflicting work is still valuable
    22→2. If valuable: re-envision against new baseline, file a bead for re-implementation
    23→3. If obsolete (e.g., touches deleted system): discard with a note
    24→4. If unclear: escalate to human/mayor
    25→
    26→Polecats can file a bead and delegate. Refinery must resolve inline.
    27→
    28→## Variables
    29→
    30→| Variable | Source | Description |
    31→|----------|--------|-------------|
    32→| role | auto-detected | Agent role (crew/polecat/refinery/witness) |
    33→| build_command | config or default | Build command (default: `go build ./...`) |
    34→| test_command | config or default | Test command (default: `go test ./...`) |
    35→
    36→## Failure Modes
    37→
    38→| Situation | Action |
    39→|-----------|--------|
    40→| Rebase conflict | Preserve work via bead, complete sync on clean state |
    41→| Uncommitted work | Stash or commit, then sync |
    42→| Untracked files | Warn, let agent decide (keep/delete/gitignore) |
    43→| bd doctor errors | Report findings, agent triages |
    44→| Test failures | File bead if not already tracked, don't block sync |
    45→| Build failure | Must be resolved before marking sync complete |"""
    46→formula = "mol-sync-workspace"
    47→version = 1
    48→
    49→[[steps]]
    50→id = "assess-state"
    51→title = "Assess current workspace state"
    52→description = """
    53→Capture current state before any changes.
    54→
    55→**0. Prime context (critical for fresh sessions):**
    56→```bash
    57→gt prime   # Load gt/mol context
    58→bd prime   # Load beads context
    59→```
    60→These ensure the agent has full system context, especially after session
    61→start, compaction, or context clear.
    62→
    63→**1. Check git status:**
    64→```bash
    65→git status --porcelain
    66→git stash list
    67→git branch --show-current
    68→```
    69→
    70→**2. Check beads state:**
    71→```bash
    72→bd sync --status
    73→```
    74→
    75→**3. Document starting state:**
    76→- Branch name
    77→- Uncommitted changes (staged/unstaged)
    78→- Untracked files (list them)
    79→- Stash entries
    80→- Beads sync status
    81→
    82→This information guides decisions in subsequent steps.
    83→
    84→**Exit criteria:** Context primed, starting state documented."""
    85→
    86→[[steps]]
    87→id = "handle-dirty-state"
    88→title = "Handle uncommitted and untracked work"
    89→needs = ["assess-state"]
    90→description = """
    91→Clean up any in-flight work before syncing.
    92→
    93→**If uncommitted changes exist:**
    94→
    95→Option A - Commit if ready:
    96→```bash
    97→git add -A && git commit -m "WIP: <description>"
    98→```
    99→
   100→Option B - Stash if not ready:
   101→```bash
   102→git stash push -m "pre-sync stash $(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
   103→```
   104→
   105→**If untracked files exist:**
   106→
   107→For each untracked file, decide:
   108→- **Keep**: Add to .gitignore or commit
   109→- **Delete**: `rm <file>`
   110→- **Stash**: Can't stash untracked directly; commit or delete
   111→
   112→**WARN the agent**: Report untracked files prominently. They may be:
   113→- WIP that should be preserved
   114→- Build artifacts that should be gitignored
   115→- Cruft that should be deleted
   116→
   117→Don't auto-delete without confirmation.
   118→
   119→**If stash entries exist:**
   120→
   121→List and assess:
   122→```bash
   123→git stash list
   124→git stash show -p stash@{0}  # Preview each
   125→```
   126→
   127→Old stashes (>1 week) are likely stale. Consider dropping.
   128→Recent stashes may contain valuable WIP - preserve or commit.
   129→
   130→**Exit criteria:** Working tree clean or explicitly stashed."""
   131→
   132→[[steps]]
   133→id = "sync-git"
   134→title = "Sync with git remote"
   135→needs = ["cleanup-worktrees"]
   136→description = """
   137→Fetch and rebase onto current main.
   138→
   139→**1. Fetch latest:**
   140→```bash
   141→git fetch origin
   142→```
   143→
   144→**2. Check divergence:**
   145→```bash
   146→git log --oneline HEAD..origin/main | head -5   # What's new on main
   147→git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD | head -5   # What we have locally
   148→```
   149→
   150→**3. Rebase onto main:**
   151→```bash
   152→git pull --rebase origin main
   153→```
   154→
   155→**If rebase succeeds:** Continue to next step.
   156→
   157→**If rebase conflicts:**
   158→
   159→The Preservation Protocol:
   160→1. **Assess the conflicting work:**
   161→   - Is it still valuable against new baseline?
   162→   - Is the conflicting area still relevant (not deleted/refactored)?
   163→
   164→2. **If work is valuable:**
   165→   ```bash
   166→   git rebase --abort
   167→   # Create a bead capturing the work
   168→   bd create --title "Re-land: <description>" --type task --priority 2
   169→   # Note the changes needed in the bead description
   170→   # Reset to clean state
   171→   git reset --hard origin/main
   172→   ```
   173→
   174→3. **If work is obsolete:**
   175→   ```bash
   176→   git rebase --abort
   177→   git reset --hard origin/main
   178→   # Note why it was discarded
   179→   ```
   180→
   181→4. **If unclear:**
   182→   - Escalate to human/mayor
   183→   - Document the conflict
   184→   - Proceed with sync on clean baseline
   185→
   186→**Refinery-specific conflict resolution:**
   187→
   188→Unlike polecats who can file a bead and delegate, the Refinery MUST resolve
   189→conflicts inline. The Refinery processes the merge queue - if it can't resolve
   190→a conflict, the queue backs up and polecats' completed work never lands.
   191→
   192→**Refinery conflict protocol:**
   193→
   194→1. **Analyze the conflict:**
   195→   ```bash
   196→   git status                    # See conflicted files
   197→   git diff                      # Examine conflict markers
   198→   ```
   199→
   200→2. **Trivial conflicts (resolve immediately):**
   201→   - Import ordering changes
   202→   - Whitespace or formatting
   203→   - Non-overlapping additions in same file
   204→   - Version bumps or changelog entries
   205→
   206→   Resolution:
   207→   ```bash
   208→   # Edit conflicted files to resolve
   209→   git add <resolved-files>
   210→   git rebase --continue
   211→   ```
   212→
   213→3. **Semantic conflicts (assess carefully):**
   214→   - Both sides modified same function
   215→   - Deleted code that the other side modified
   216→   - Structural changes (renamed files, moved code)
   217→
   218→   If you can understand both intents and merge correctly:
   219→   ```bash
   220→   # Carefully merge the changes, preserving both intents
   221→   git add <resolved-files>
   222→   git rebase --continue
   223→   ```
   224→
   225→4. **Complex conflicts (abort and notify polecat):**
   226→   - Conflicting business logic changes
   227→   - Unclear which version is correct
   228→   - Risk of subtle bugs from incorrect merge
   229→
   230→   ```bash
   231→   git rebase --abort
   232→   gt mail send {{ rig }}/polecats/<worker> -s "Rebase needed" \
   233→     -m "Your branch conflicts with main in <files>. Please rebase and resubmit via gt done."
   234→   # Skip this branch, continue with queue
   235→   ```
   236→
   237→**The Refinery judgment call:** When in doubt, abort and notify rather than
   238→risk merging incorrectly. A resubmitted branch is better than a broken main.
   239→
   240→**Exit criteria:** Local branch matches or is cleanly ahead of origin/main."""
   241→
   242→[[steps]]
   243→id = "sync-beads"
   244→title = "Sync beads state"
   245→needs = ["sync-git"]
   246→description = """
   247→Sync the beads database with remote.
   248→
   249→```bash
   250→bd sync
   251→```
   252→
   253→**If conflicts:**
   254→Beads uses JSONL append-only format, so true conflicts are rare.
   255→If they occur:
   256→```bash
   257→bd sync --status  # Diagnose
   258→```
   259→
   260→Likely causes:
   261→- Two agents edited same bead simultaneously
   262→- Corrupted beads-sync branch
   263→
   264→Resolution:
   265→```bash
   266→# Try pulling fresh
   267→git fetch origin beads-sync
   268→bd sync
   269→```
   270→
   271→If still failing, escalate to mayor.
   272→
   273→**Exit criteria:** Beads synced successfully."""
   274→
   275→[[steps]]
   276→id = "run-doctor"
   277→title = "Run beads health check"
   278→needs = ["sync-beads"]
   279→description = """
   280→Check for beads system issues.
   281→
   282→```bash
   283→bd doctor
   284→```
   285→
   286→**Triage findings:**
   287→
   288→| Finding | Action |
   289→|---------|--------|
   290→| Orphaned issues (committed but not closed) | Review and close if complete |
   291→| Missing dependencies | Fix with `bd dep add` |
   292→| Circular dependencies | Resolve or escalate |
   293→| Stale in_progress | Check if still active, update status |
   294→| Route misconfigurations | Fix routes.jsonl |
   295→
   296→**For each issue found:**
   297→- Quick fix (<5 min): Fix now
   298→- Medium fix: File a bead
   299→- Unclear: Note for human review
   300→
   301→**Exit criteria:** Doctor findings triaged (not necessarily all fixed)."""
   302→
   303→[[steps]]
   304→id = "verify-build"
   305→title = "Verify project builds"
   306→needs = ["run-doctor"]
   307→description = """
   308→Ensure the codebase compiles.
   309→
   310→```bash
   311→{{ build_command }}
   312→# Default: go build ./...
   313→# Configure via build_command variable for non-Go projects
   314→```
   315→
   316→**If build succeeds:** Continue to next step.
   317→
   318→**If build fails:**
   319→
   320→This is a blocking issue. Options:
   321→
   322→1. **Quick fix (<15 min):** Fix it now
   323→   ```bash
   324→   # Fix the issue
   325→   git add <files>
   326→   git commit -m "fix: build failure from sync"
   327→   git push
   328→   ```
   329→
   330→2. **Non-trivial fix:** File and escalate
   331→   ```bash
   332→   bd create --title "Build broken on main" --type bug --priority 0
   333→   gt mail send --human -s "ALERT: Main doesn't build" -m "Details..."
   334→   ```
   335→   Cannot mark sync complete with broken build.
   336→
   337→**Exit criteria:** Project builds successfully."""
   338→
   339→[[steps]]
   340→id = "run-tests"
   341→title = "Run test suite"
   342→needs = ["verify-build"]
   343→description = """
   344→Verify tests pass on current baseline.
   345→
   346→```bash
   347→{{ test_command }}
   348→# Default: go test ./...
   349→# Configure via test_command variable for non-Go projects
   350→```
   351→
   352→**If tests pass:** Continue to next step.
   353→
   354→**If tests fail:**
   355→
   356→Unlike build failures, test failures don't block sync completion.
   357→However, they must be tracked.
   358→
   359→1. **Check if already tracked:**
   360→   ```bash
   361→   bd list --type=bug --status=open | grep -i test
   362→   ```
   363→
   364→2. **If not tracked, file a bead:**
   365→   ```bash
   366→   bd create --title "Test failure: <test name>" --type bug --priority 1
   367→   ```
   368→
   369→3. **Report in summary:**
   370→   Note which tests are failing so batch assignment accounts for it.
   371→
   372→**Exit criteria:** Test results documented, failures tracked in beads."""
   373→
   374→[[steps]]
   375→id = "cleanup-worktrees"
   376→title = "Clean up stale worktrees"
   377→needs = ["handle-dirty-state"]
   378→description = """
   379→Remove orphaned worktrees from cross-rig work.
   380→
   381→**1. List worktrees:**
   382→```bash
   383→git worktree list
   384→```
   385→
   386→**2. Identify stale worktrees:**
   387→- Worktrees for branches that no longer exist on remote
   388→- Worktrees older than 1 week without recent commits
   389→- Worktrees for completed/merged work
   390→
   391→**3. Remove stale worktrees:**
   392→```bash
   393→git worktree remove <path>
   394→# Or force if needed:
   395→git worktree remove --force <path>
   396→```
   397→
   398→**4. Prune worktree metadata:**
   399→```bash
   400→git worktree prune
   401→```
   402→
   403→**CAUTION:** Don't remove worktrees for active cross-rig work.
   404→Check with `gt worktree list` if available.
   405→
   406→**Exit criteria:** Stale worktrees removed."""
   407→
   408→[[steps]]
   409→id = "generate-report"
   410→title = "Generate sync report"
   411→needs = ["run-tests"]
   412→description = """
   413→Summarize sync results for broadcast response.
   414→
   415→**Git status check:**
   416→```bash
   417→# Get current branch
   418→BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
   419→
   420→# Check divergence from origin/main
   421→BEHIND=$(git rev-list HEAD..origin/main --count)
   422→AHEAD=$(git rev-list origin/main..HEAD --count)
   423→```
   424→
   425→**Report format (for feature branches):**
   426→```
   427→SYNC COMPLETE: <agent-name>
   428→
   429→Git:
   430→  - Branch: <branch>
   431→  - Commits behind main: <n>
   432→  - Commits ahead of main: <n>
   433→  - Conflicts resolved: <y/n, details if yes>
   434→
   435→Beads:
   436→  - Sync status: OK
   437→  - Doctor findings: <count> (fixed: <n>, filed: <n>, deferred: <n>)
   438→
   439→Build: PASS
   440→Tests: PASS | FAIL (<count> failures, tracked in <bead-ids>)
   441→
   442→Worktrees cleaned: <count>
   443→
   444→Ready for work: YES | NO (<reason>)
   445→```
   446→
   447→**Report format (for crew on main):**
   448→
   449→When the agent is on the main branch directly (common for crew workers),
   450→use "Unpushed commits" instead of "Commits ahead of main":
   451→
   452→```
   453→SYNC COMPLETE: <agent-name>
   454→
   455→Git:
   456→  - Branch: main
   457→  - Commits behind origin: <n>
   458→  - Unpushed commits: <n>
   459→  - Conflicts resolved: <y/n, details if yes>
   460→...
   461→```
   462→
   463→This distinction is important because:
   464→- Crew workers often work directly on main
   465→- "Commits ahead of main" is confusing when you ARE on main
   466→- "Unpushed commits" is clearer - it means local work not yet on origin
   467→
   468→**Exit criteria:** Report generated."""
   469→
   470→[[steps]]
   471→id = "signal-ready"
   472→title = "Signal readiness"
   473→needs = ["generate-report"]
   474→description = """
   475→Report sync completion to coordinator (if broadcast) or to self (if autonomous).
   476→
   477→**If responding to broadcast:**
   478→```bash
   479→gt mail send <coordinator> -s "SYNC_COMPLETE $(hostname)" -m "<report>"
   480→```
   481→
   482→**If autonomous sync:**
   483→- Log the report
   484→- Update any relevant agent status
   485→
   486→**If sync failed (couldn't build):**
   487→```bash
   488→gt mail send --human -s "SYNC_FAILED $(hostname)" -m "<report with failure details>"
   489→```
   490→
   491→**Exit criteria:** Readiness signaled, molecule complete."""
   492→

1→description = """
     2→Feed stranded convoys by dispatching ready work to available polecats.
     3→
     4→Dogs execute this formula when the Deacon detects a stranded convoy. A convoy
     5→is stranded when it has ready issues (open, unblocked, no assignee) but no
     6→workers are processing them.
     7→
     8→## Dog Contract
     9→
    10→This is infrastructure work. You:
    11→1. Receive convoy ID via variable
    12→2. Load convoy and find ready issues
    13→3. Check idle polecat capacity across rigs
    14→4. Dispatch min(ready_issues, idle_polecats) using gt sling
    15→5. Report actions taken
    16→6. Return to kennel
    17→
    18→## Variables
    19→
    20→| Variable | Source | Description |
    21→|----------|--------|-------------|
    22→| convoy | --var | The stranded convoy ID to feed |
    23→
    24→## Single Pass Design
    25→
    26→Dog doesn't babysit or wait for completion. The workflow is:
    27→1. Find ready issues in convoy
    28→2. Dispatch each to an available polecat
    29→3. Exit immediately
    30→
    31→If convoy is still stranded next Deacon patrol cycle, another dog will be
    32→dispatched. This keeps the system stateless and batch-oriented.
    33→
    34→## Failure Modes
    35→
    36→| Situation | Action |
    37→|-----------|--------|
    38→| Convoy not found | Exit with error, notify Deacon |
    39→| No ready issues | Exit success (false positive, convoy is fine) |
    40→| No idle polecats | Exit success, note in report (will retry next cycle) |
    41→| Sling fails | Continue with remaining issues, note failures |"""
    42→formula = "mol-convoy-feed"
    43→version = 1
    44→
    45→[squash]
    46→trigger = "on_complete"
    47→template_type = "work"
    48→include_metrics = true
    49→
    50→[[steps]]
    51→id = "load-convoy"
    52→title = "Load convoy and identify ready issues"
    53→description = """
    54→Load the convoy and find issues ready for dispatch.
    55→
    56→**1. Check assignment:**
    57→```bash
    58→gt hook               # Shows convoy in hook_bead or vars
    59→```
    60→
    61→**2. Load convoy details:**
    62→```bash
    63→gt convoy status {{convoy}} --json
    64→```
    65→
    66→**3. Identify ready issues:**
    67→
    68→For each tracked issue in the convoy:
    69→```bash
    70→bd show <issue-id> --json
    71→```
    72→
    73→An issue is "ready" if ALL of these are true:
    74→- status = "open" (NOT in_progress, closed, or hooked)
    75→- not in blocked list (check: bd blocked --json)
    76→- assignee is empty OR assignee session is dead
    77→
    78→Check blocked status:
    79→```bash
    80→bd blocked --json
    81→# If issue ID appears here, it's blocked (skip it)
    82→```
    83→
    84→Check assignee session if set:
    85→```bash
    86→# If assignee like "gastown/polecats/nux"
    87→tmux has-session -t gt-gastown-polecat-nux 2>/dev/null && echo "alive" || echo "dead"
    88→```
    89→
    90→**4. Build ready list:**
    91→Collect all ready issues with their metadata:
    92→- Issue ID
    93→- Title
    94→- Priority
    95→- Rig (extracted from prefix)
    96→
    97→Sort by priority (P0 first) for dispatch order.
    98→
    99→**Exit criteria:** Ready issues identified and prioritized."""
   100→
   101→[[steps]]
   102→id = "check-capacity"
   103→title = "Check polecat capacity across rigs"
   104→needs = ["load-convoy"]
   105→description = """
   106→Determine how many polecats are available for dispatch.
   107→
   108→**1. For each rig that has ready issues:**
   109→```bash
   110→gt polecats <rig>
   111→# Shows polecat status: idle, working, etc.
   112→```
   113→
   114→**2. Count available capacity:**
   115→Available polecats are those that:
   116→- Exist in the rig's polecat pool
   117→- Currently idle (no hooked work)
   118→- Session is running
   119→
   120→**3. Calculate dispatch count:**
   121→```
   122→dispatch_count = min(ready_issues, available_polecats)
   123→```
   124→
   125→If dispatch_count = 0:
   126→- Log: "No capacity available, will retry next cycle"
   127→- Proceed to report step (no dispatches to make)
   128→
   129→**4. Match issues to rigs:**
   130→For each ready issue, determine target rig from issue prefix:
   131→- gt-* issues → gastown rig
   132→- bd-* issues → beads rig
   133→- etc.
   134→
   135→**Exit criteria:** Dispatch plan created with issue→rig mappings."""
   136→
   137→[[steps]]
   138→id = "dispatch-work"
   139→title = "Dispatch ready issues to polecats"
   140→needs = ["check-capacity"]
   141→description = """
   142→Sling each ready issue to an available polecat.
   143→
   144→**For each issue in dispatch plan:**
   145→
   146→```bash
   147→# Dispatch issue to the appropriate rig
   148→# This spawns a fresh polecat or assigns to idle one
   149→gt sling <issue-id> <rig>
   150→
   151→# Example:
   152→gt sling gt-abc123 gastown
   153→gt sling bd-xyz789 beads
   154→```
   155→
   156→**Track results:**
   157→For each dispatch:
   158→- Success: Note issue ID, target rig, polecat assigned
   159→- Failure: Note issue ID, error message
   160→
   161→**Important notes:**
   162→- `gt sling` handles polecat selection automatically
   163→- It will spawn a new polecat if none available
   164→- The polecat gets the issue hooked and starts immediately
   165→- Don't wait for polecat to complete - fire and forget
   166→
   167→**If sling fails:**
   168→- Continue with remaining issues
   169→- Note the failure for the report
   170→- Don't escalate individual failures (will retry next cycle)
   171→
   172→**Exit criteria:** All dispatchable issues have been slung."""
   173→
   174→[[steps]]
   175→id = "report-results"
   176→title = "Generate and send feeding report"
   177→needs = ["dispatch-work"]
   178→description = """
   179→Create summary report of convoy feeding actions.
   180→
   181→**1. Generate report:**
   182→```markdown
   183→## Convoy Feed Report: {{convoy}}
   184→
   185→**Ready issues found**: {{ready_count}}
   186→**Polecats available**: {{available_count}}
   187→**Issues dispatched**: {{dispatch_count}}
   188→
   189→### Dispatched Work
   190→{{#each dispatched}}
   191→- {{issue_id}}: {{title}} → {{rig}}/{{polecat}}
   192→{{/each}}
   193→
   194→### Skipped (no capacity)
   195→{{#if skipped}}
   196→{{#each skipped}}
   197→- {{issue_id}}: {{title}} (will retry next cycle)
   198→{{/each}}
   199→{{else}}
   200→(none)
   201→{{/if}}
   202→
   203→### Errors
   204→{{#if errors}}
   205→{{#each errors}}
   206→- {{issue_id}}: {{error}}
   207→{{/each}}
   208→{{else}}
   209→(none)
   210→{{/if}}
   211→```
   212→
   213→**2. Send to Deacon:**
   214→```bash
   215→gt mail send deacon/ -s "Convoy fed: {{convoy}}" -m "$(cat <<EOF
   216→Convoy {{convoy}} feeding complete.
   217→
   218→Dispatched: {{dispatch_count}}/{{ready_count}} issues
   219→{{#if errors}}Errors: {{error_count}}{{/if}}
   220→
   221→{{report_summary}}
   222→EOF
   223→)"
   224→```
   225→
   226→**3. Update convoy (optional):**
   227→If convoy has a notify field, could add a note about feeding activity.
   228→Not required - the dispatch tracking handles visibility.
   229→
   230→**Exit criteria:** Report generated and sent."""
   231→
   232→[[steps]]
   233→id = "return-to-kennel"
   234→title = "Signal completion and return to kennel"
   235→needs = ["report-results"]
   236→description = """
   237→Signal work complete and return to available pool.
   238→
   239→**1. Signal completion to Deacon:**
   240→```bash
   241→gt mail send deacon/ -s "DOG_DONE $(hostname)" -m "Task: convoy-feed
   242→Convoy: {{convoy}}
   243→Ready: {{ready_count}}
   244→Dispatched: {{dispatch_count}}
   245→Status: COMPLETE
   246→
   247→Ready for next assignment."
   248→```
   249→
   250→**2. Return to kennel:**
   251→Dog returns to available state in the pool. Deacon will assign next work
   252→or retire the dog if pool is oversized.
   253→
   254→**Exit criteria:** Deacon notified, dog ready for next work or retirement."""
   255→
   256→[vars]
   257→[vars.convoy]
   258→description = "The convoy ID to feed"
   259→required = true
   260→

1→description = """
     2→Archive completed convoys and notify overseer.
     3→
     4→Dogs work through molecules (poured from this formula) when convoys complete. The Deacon detects completed
     5→convoys (all tracked issues closed) and slings this work to a dog for:
     6→- Generating convoy summary
     7→- Archiving convoy state
     8→- Notifying the overseer (Mayor)
     9→- Updating activity feed
    10→
    11→## Dog Contract
    12→
    13→This is infrastructure work. You:
    14→1. Receive convoy ID via hook_bead
    15→2. Generate summary of completed work
    16→3. Archive to appropriate location
    17→4. Notify stakeholders
    18→5. Return to kennel
    19→
    20→## Variables
    21→
    22→| Variable | Source | Description |
    23→|----------|--------|-------------|
    24→| convoy | hook_bead | The convoy ID to archive |
    25→
    26→## Failure Modes
    27→
    28→| Situation | Action |
    29→|-----------|--------|
    30→| Convoy not found | Exit with error, notify Deacon |
    31→| Archive location full | Create space, retry, or escalate |
    32→| Mail send fails | Retry once, then proceed anyway |"""
    33→formula = "mol-convoy-cleanup"
    34→version = 1
    35→
    36→[squash]
    37→trigger = "on_complete"
    38→template_type = "work"
    39→include_metrics = true
    40→
    41→[[steps]]
    42→id = "load-convoy"
    43→title = "Load convoy and verify completion"
    44→description = """
    45→Load the convoy bead and verify it's ready for archival.
    46→
    47→**1. Check your assignment:**
    48→```bash
    49→gt hook               # Shows hook_bead = convoy ID
    50→bd show {{convoy}}          # Full convoy details
    51→```
    52→
    53→**2. Verify convoy is complete:**
    54→- Status should be 'closed' or all tracked issues closed
    55→- If convoy is still open, exit - Deacon dispatched too early
    56→
    57→```bash
    58→bd show {{convoy}}
    59→# Check 'tracks' or 'dependencies' field
    60→# All tracked issues should be closed
    61→```
    62→
    63→**3. Gather convoy metadata:**
    64→- Start date (created_at)
    65→- End date (last closure timestamp)
    66→- Total issues tracked
    67→- Contributing polecats
    68→
    69→**Exit criteria:** Convoy loaded, verified complete, metadata gathered."""
    70→
    71→[[steps]]
    72→id = "generate-summary"
    73→title = "Generate convoy summary"
    74→needs = ["load-convoy"]
    75→description = """
    76→Create a summary of the convoy's completed work.
    77→
    78→**1. Collect tracked issue details:**
    79→```bash
    80→# For each tracked issue
    81→bd show <tracked-id>
    82→# Extract: title, type, assignee, duration
    83→```
    84→
    85→**2. Calculate statistics:**
    86→- Total duration (convoy start to finish)
    87→- Issues by type (task, bug, feature)
    88→- Contributors (unique assignees)
    89→- Commits generated (if tracked)
    90→
    91→**3. Create summary text:**
    92→```markdown
    93→## Convoy Summary: {{convoy.title}}
    94→
    95→**Duration**: X days/hours
    96→**Issues completed**: N
    97→
    98→### Work Breakdown
    99→- Tasks: N
   100→- Bugs: N
   101→- Features: N
   102→
   103→### Contributors
   104→- polecat-1: N issues
   105→- polecat-2: N issues
   106→
   107→### Key Outcomes
   108→- <notable achievement 1>
   109→- <notable achievement 2>
   110→```
   111→
   112→**Exit criteria:** Summary text generated and ready for notification."""
   113→
   114→[[steps]]
   115→id = "archive-convoy"
   116→title = "Archive convoy to cold storage"
   117→needs = ["generate-summary"]
   118→description = """
   119→Move convoy from active to archived state.
   120→
   121→**1. Update convoy status:**
   122→```bash
   123→bd update {{convoy}} --status=archived
   124→# Or close if not already closed
   125→bd close {{convoy}} --reason="Convoy complete, archived"
   126→```
   127→
   128→**2. Generate archive record:**
   129→The convoy bead with all metadata is the archive record. Beads retention
   130→handles moving it to `.beads/archive/` after the retention period.
   131→
   132→**3. Verify archive:**
   133→```bash
   134→bd show {{convoy}}
   135→# Status should reflect archived state
   136→```
   137→
   138→**4. Sync to persist:**
   139→```bash
   140→bd sync
   141→```
   142→
   143→**Exit criteria:** Convoy archived, changes synced."""
   144→
   145→[[steps]]
   146→id = "notify-overseer"
   147→title = "Send completion notification to overseer"
   148→needs = ["archive-convoy"]
   149→description = """
   150→Notify the Mayor (overseer) of convoy completion.
   151→
   152→**1. Send completion mail:**
   153→```bash
   154→gt mail send mayor/ -s "Convoy complete: {{convoy.title}}" -m "$(cat <<EOF
   155→Convoy {{convoy}} has completed and been archived.
   156→
   157→## Summary
   158→{{generated_summary}}
   159→
   160→## Metrics
   161→- Duration: {{duration}}
   162→- Issues: {{issue_count}}
   163→- Contributors: {{contributor_list}}
   164→
   165→This convoy has been archived. View details: bd show {{convoy}}
   166→EOF
   167→)"
   168→```
   169→
   170→**2. Post to activity feed:**
   171→The convoy closure already creates a feed entry. Verify:
   172→```bash
   173→gt feed --since 5m
   174→# Should show convoy completion
   175→```
   176→
   177→**Exit criteria:** Overseer notified via mail, activity feed updated."""
   178→
   179→[[steps]]
   180→id = "return-to-kennel"
   181→title = "Signal completion and return to kennel"
   182→needs = ["notify-overseer"]
   183→description = """
   184→Signal work complete and return to available pool.
   185→
   186→**1. Signal completion to Deacon:**
   187→```bash
   188→gt mail send deacon/ -s "DOG_DONE $(hostname)" -m "Task: convoy-cleanup
   189→Convoy: {{convoy}}
   190→Status: COMPLETE
   191→Duration: {{work_duration}}
   192→
   193→Ready for next assignment."
   194→```
   195→
   196→**2. Clean workspace:**
   197→- No convoy-specific state to clean
   198→- Workspace should already be clean
   199→
   200→**3. Return to kennel:**
   201→Dog returns to available state in the pool. Deacon will assign next work
   202→or retire the dog if pool is oversized.
   203→
   204→**Exit criteria:** Deacon notified, dog ready for next work or retirement."""
   205→
   206→[vars]
   207→[vars.convoy]
   208→description = "The convoy ID to archive"
   209→required = true
   210→

1→description = """
     2→Propagate cross-rig dependency resolution.
     3→
     4→Dogs work through molecules (poured from this formula) when dependencies resolve across rig boundaries.
     5→When an issue in one rig closes, dependent issues in other rigs may unblock.
     6→This formula handles:
     7→- Finding cross-rig dependents
     8→- Notifying affected rigs
     9→- Updating blocked status
    10→- Triggering work dispatch if appropriate
    11→
    12→## Dog Contract
    13→
    14→This is infrastructure work. You:
    15→1. Receive closed issue ID via hook_bead
    16→2. Find all cross-rig dependents (issues in other rigs blocked by this)
    17→3. Notify affected Witnesses
    18→4. Optionally trigger dispatch if issues are now ready
    19→5. Return to kennel
    20→
    21→## Variables
    22→
    23→| Variable | Source | Description |
    24→|----------|--------|-------------|
    25→| resolved_issue | hook_bead | The issue that just closed |
    26→
    27→## Why Dogs?
    28→
    29→Cross-rig work requires multi-rig worktrees. Dogs have these, polecats don't.
    30→The Deacon detects the closure, but the propagation needs rig access."""
    31→formula = "mol-dep-propagate"
    32→version = 1
    33→
    34→[squash]
    35→trigger = "on_complete"
    36→template_type = "work"
    37→include_metrics = true
    38→
    39→[[steps]]
    40→id = "load-resolved-issue"
    41→title = "Load resolved issue and find dependents"
    42→description = """
    43→Load the closed issue and identify cross-rig dependents.
    44→
    45→**1. Check your assignment:**
    46→```bash
    47→gt hook               # Shows hook_bead = resolved issue ID
    48→bd show {{resolved_issue}}  # Full issue details
    49→```
    50→
    51→**2. Verify issue is closed:**
    52→```bash
    53→bd show {{resolved_issue}}
    54→# Status should be 'closed' or similar terminal state
    55→```
    56→
    57→**3. Find dependents (issues blocked by this one):**
    58→```bash
    59→bd show {{resolved_issue}}
    60→# Look at 'blocks' field - these are issues waiting on this one
    61→```
    62→
    63→**4. Identify cross-rig dependents:**
    64→- Same-rig dependents: Already handled by local beads (automatic unblock)
    65→- Cross-rig dependents: Different prefix (e.g., gt- vs bd-) need propagation
    66→
    67→```bash
    68→# Example: resolved_issue is bd-xxx, blocks gt-yyy
    69→# gt-yyy is cross-rig and needs notification
    70→```
    71→
    72→**Exit criteria:** Resolved issue loaded, cross-rig dependents identified."""
    73→
    74→[[steps]]
    75→id = "update-blocked-status"
    76→title = "Update blocked status in affected rigs"
    77→needs = ["load-resolved-issue"]
    78→description = """
    79→Update the blocked status for cross-rig dependents.
    80→
    81→**1. For each cross-rig dependent:**
    82→```bash
    83→# Navigate to the rig containing the dependent issue
    84→# Dogs have multi-rig worktrees for this
    85→
    86→bd show <dependent-id>
    87→# Check if this was the only blocker
    88→```
    89→
    90→**2. Check if now unblocked:**
    91→```bash
    92→bd blocked <dependent-id>
    93→# If empty or only shows other blockers, issue is now unblocked
    94→```
    95→
    96→**3. Verify automatic unblock worked:**
    97→Beads should auto-update blocked status when dependencies close.
    98→This step verifies and fixes if needed:
    99→```bash
   100→# If still showing as blocked by resolved issue (shouldn't happen):
   101→bd dep remove <dependent-id> {{resolved_issue}}
   102→```
   103→
   104→**Exit criteria:** All cross-rig dependents have updated blocked status."""
   105→
   106→[[steps]]
   107→id = "notify-witnesses"
   108→title = "Notify affected rig Witnesses"
   109→needs = ["update-blocked-status"]
   110→description = """
   111→Send notifications to Witnesses of affected rigs.
   112→
   113→**1. Group dependents by rig:**
   114→- gastown/witness: for gt-* issues
   115→- beads/witness: for bd-* issues
   116→- etc.
   117→
   118→**2. For each affected rig, send notification:**
   119→```bash
   120→gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "Dependency resolved: {{resolved_issue}}" -m "$(cat <<EOF
   121→External dependency has closed, unblocking work in your rig.
   122→
   123→## Resolved Issue
   124→- ID: {{resolved_issue}}
   125→- Title: {{resolved_issue.title}}
   126→- Rig: {{resolved_issue.prefix}}
   127→
   128→## Unblocked in Your Rig
   129→{{range dependent}}
   130→- {{dependent.id}}: {{dependent.title}} ({{dependent.status}})
   131→{{end}}
   132→
   133→These issues may now proceed. Check bd ready for available work.
   134→EOF
   135→)"
   136→```
   137→
   138→**3. Log notification:**
   139→Note which Witnesses were notified for audit trail.
   140→
   141→**Exit criteria:** All affected Witnesses notified."""
   142→
   143→[[steps]]
   144→id = "trigger-dispatch"
   145→title = "Optionally trigger work dispatch"
   146→needs = ["notify-witnesses"]
   147→description = """
   148→Trigger work dispatch for newly-unblocked issues if appropriate.
   149→
   150→**1. For each unblocked issue, check if ready for work:**
   151→```bash
   152→bd show <issue-id>
   153→# Check:
   154→# - Status: should be 'open' (not already in_progress)
   155→# - Priority: high priority may warrant immediate dispatch
   156→# - No other blockers: bd blocked should be empty
   157→```
   158→
   159→**2. Decision: trigger dispatch?**
   160→
   161→| Condition | Action |
   162→|-----------|--------|
   163→| High priority (P0-P1) + open + unblocked | Recommend immediate dispatch |
   164→| Medium priority (P2) + open + unblocked | Note in Witness notification |
   165→| Low priority (P3-P4) | Let Witness handle in next patrol |
   166→
   167→**3. If triggering dispatch:**
   168→```bash
   169→# For high priority, suggest to Mayor:
   170→gt mail send mayor/ -s "High-priority work unblocked: <issue>" -m "..."
   171→```
   172→
   173→Usually, the Witness notification (previous step) is sufficient - Witnesses
   174→handle their own dispatch decisions.
   175→
   176→**Exit criteria:** Dispatch recommendations sent where appropriate."""
   177→
   178→[[steps]]
   179→id = "return-to-kennel"
   180→title = "Signal completion and return to kennel"
   181→needs = ["trigger-dispatch"]
   182→description = """
   183→Signal work complete and return to available pool.
   184→
   185→**1. Signal completion to Deacon:**
   186→```bash

1→description = """
     2→Generate daily digest for overseer (Mayor).
     3→
     4→Dogs work through molecules (poured from this formula) on a scheduled basis (daily, or triggered by plugin)
     5→to create summary digests of Gas Town activity. This aggregates:
     6→- Work completed across all rigs
     7→- Issues filed and closed
     8→- Incidents and escalations
     9→- Agent health metrics
    10→- Key statistics and trends
    11→
    12→## Dog Contract
    13→
    14→This is infrastructure work. You:
    15→1. Receive digest period via hook_bead (e.g., daily, weekly)
    16→2. Collect data from all rigs you have access to
    17→3. Generate formatted digest
    18→4. Send to overseer
    19→5. Archive digest as bead
    20→6. Return to kennel
    21→
    22→## Variables
    23→
    24→| Variable | Source | Description |
    25→|----------|--------|-------------|
    26→| period | hook_bead | Time period for digest (daily, weekly) |
    27→| since | computed | Start timestamp for data collection |
    28→| until | computed | End timestamp (usually now) |
    29→
    30→## Why Dogs?
    31→
    32→Digest generation requires reading from multiple rigs. Dogs have multi-rig
    33→worktrees. This is also a periodic task that doesn't need a dedicated polecat."""
    34→formula = "mol-digest-generate"
    35→version = 1
    36→
    37→[squash]
    38→trigger = "on_complete"
    39→template_type = "work"
    40→include_metrics = true
    41→
    42→[[steps]]
    43→id = "determine-period"
    44→title = "Determine digest time period"
    45→description = """
    46→Establish the time range for this digest.

    48→**1. Check assignment:**
    49→```bash
    50→gt hook               # Shows period type
    51→```
    52→
    53→**2. Calculate time range:**
    54→
    55→| Period | Since | Until |
    56→|--------|-------|-------|
    57→| daily | Yesterday 00:00 | Today 00:00 |
    58→| weekly | Last Monday 00:00 | This Monday 00:00 |
    59→| custom | From hook_bead | From hook_bead |
    60→
    61→```bash
    62→# For daily digest
    63→since=$(date -v-1d +%Y-%m-%dT00:00:00)
    64→until=$(date +%Y-%m-%dT00:00:00)
    65→```
    66→
    67→**3. Record period for reporting:**
    68→Note the exact timestamps for the digest header.
    69→
    70→**Exit criteria:** Time period established with precise timestamps."""
    71→
    72→[[steps]]
    73→id = "collect-rig-data"

    83→```
    84→
    85→**2. For each rig, collect:**

    87→a) **Issues filed and closed:**
    88→```bash
    89→# From rig beads

    91→bd list --status=closed --updated-after={{since}}
    92→```
    93→
    94→b) **Agent activity:**
    95→```bash
    96→gt polecats <rig>           # Polecat activity
    97→gt feed --since={{since}}   # Activity feed entries
    98→```
    99→
   100→c) **Merges:**
   101→```bash
   102→# Git log for merges to main
   103→git -C <rig-path> log --merges --since={{since}} --oneline main
   104→```
   105→
   106→d) **Incidents:**
   107→```bash
   108→# Issues tagged as incident or high-priority
   109→bd list --label=incident --created-after={{since}}
   110→```
   111→
   112→**3. Aggregate across rigs:**
   113→Sum counts, collect notable items, identify trends.
   114→
   115→**Exit criteria:** Raw data collected from all accessible rigs."""
   116→
   117→[[steps]]
   118→id = "generate-digest"

   121→description = """
   122→Transform collected data into formatted digest.

   124→**1. Calculate summary statistics:**
   125→- Total issues filed

   129→- By rig
   130→
   131→**2. Identify highlights:**
   132→- Biggest completions (epics, large features)
   133→- Incidents (any P0/P1 issues)
   134→- Notable trends (increasing backlog, fast closure rate)
   135→
   136→**3. Generate digest text:**
   137→```markdown
   138→# Gas Town Daily Digest: {{date}}
   139→
   140→## Summary
   141→- **Issues filed**: N (tasks: X, bugs: Y, features: Z)
   142→- **Issues closed**: N
   143→- **Net change**: +/-N
   144→
   145→## By Rig
   146→| Rig | Filed | Closed | Active Polecats |
   147→|-----|-------|--------|-----------------|
   148→| gastown | X | Y | Z |
   149→| beads | X | Y | Z |
   150→
   151→## Highlights
   152→### Completed
   153→- {{epic or feature}} - completed by {{polecat}}
   154→
   155→### Incidents
   156→- {{incident summary if any}}
   157→
   158→## Agent Health
   159→- Polecats spawned: N
   160→- Polecats retired: N
   161→- Average work duration: Xh
   162→
   163→## Trends
   164→- Backlog: {{increasing/stable/decreasing}}
   165→- Throughput: {{issues/day}}
   166→```
   167→
   168→**Exit criteria:** Formatted digest ready for delivery."""

   170→[[steps]]

   184→
   185→**2. Archive as bead:**
   186→Create a digest bead for permanent record:
   187→```bash
   188→bd create --title="Digest: {{date}}" --type=digest \
   189→  --description="{{formatted_digest}}" \
   190→  --label=digest,{{period}}
   191→```
   192→
   193→**3. Sync:**
   194→```bash
   195→bd sync
   196→```
   197→
   198→**Exit criteria:** Digest sent to Mayor and archived as bead."""
   199→
   200→[[steps]]
   201→id = "return-to-kennel"
   202→title = "Signal completion and return to kennel"
   203→needs = ["send-digest"]
   204→description = """
   205→Signal work complete and return to available pool.
   206→
   207→**1. Signal completion to Deacon:**
   208→```bash
   209→gt mail send deacon/ -s "DOG_DONE $(hostname)" -m "Task: digest-generate
   210→Period: {{period}}
   211→Date range: {{since}} to {{until}}
   212→Status: COMPLETE
   213→
   214→Digest sent to Mayor.
   215→Ready for next assignment."
   216→```
   217→
   218→**2. Return to kennel:**
   219→Dog returns to available state in the pool.
   220→
   221→**Exit criteria:** Deacon notified, dog ready for next work."""
   222→
   223→[vars]
   224→[vars.period]
   225→description = "The digest period type (daily, weekly, custom)"
   226→required = true
   227→default = "daily"
